perturbation.original_text: 2. "Confidential Information" shall be deemed to include the source and object code computer programs and associated documentation, manuals and other printed or visually acceptable materials describing the use or design of software and strategic plans as well as any other information, oral or written, which shall be so noted on its face as being confidential or proprietary to the disclosing party.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. "Confidential Information" shall be deemed to include the source and object code computer programs and associated documentation.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'manuals and other printed or visually acceptable materials describing the use or design of software and strategic plans as well as any other information, oral or written, which shall be so noted on its face as being confidential or proprietary to the disclosing party.' from the definition of Confidential Information, it limits the scope significantly. This creates a contradiction if other clauses rely on a broader understanding of what constitutes Confidential Information, such as clause 5 referring to 'other information provided'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: At the termination of this examination, both parties agree to return to each other all of the documents and other information provided in connection with this examination, and all copies thereof, as soon as requested by the other party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly narrows the definition of 'Confidential Information' by removing 'manuals and other printed or visually acceptable materials describing the use or design of software and strategic plans as well as any other information, oral or written, which shall be so noted on its face as being confidential or proprietary to the disclosing party.' The contradicted text requires 'all of the documents and other information provided' to be returned. Because the modified text no longer includes certain types of information (like strategic plans or unmarked oral information) within the definition of Confidential Information, it creates a contradiction because the original intent to return all provided information is undermined, directly impacting the return obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Phoenix and Company warrant that they have the unqualified right to disclose fully the Confidential Information disclosed hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: Phoenix and Company make no guarantees regarding the Confidential Information disclosed hereunder.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the warranty to a statement of 'no guarantees' directly contradicts the earlier clauses where the parties agree to protect and treat information as confidential, and that warrant they have the rights to disclose it fully. This creates uncertainty about each party's responsibilities.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Phoenix and Company agree that they shall hold in confidence and shall not disclose any Confidential Information (as defined in Paragraph 2 below) without the prior written authorization from a corporate officer of the party to whom the information belongs nor use such Confidential Information for any purpose other than that contemplated by this Agreement. This obligation, however, shall not extend to any of the following: A. Confidential Information which at the time of disclosure is in the public domain; and B. Confidential Information which after generation or disclosure is published or otherwise becomes part of the public domain through no fault of the disclosing party (but only after and to the extent that it is published or otherwise becomes part of the public domain); C. Confidential Information which either party can show was in its possession at the time of generation or disclosure and was not acquired, directly or indirectly, from the other party or from a third party under obligation of confidence; D. Confidential Information which was received after the time of generation or disclosure hereunder, from a third party who did not require that party to hold it in confidence and who did not acquire it, directly or indirectly, form the other party under an obligation of confidence; and <PAGE> 2 E. Confidential Information which Phoenix and Company can show was developed independently without benefit of, or based on information generated hereunder or made available by the other party.
perturbation.justification: The original text provides a warranty regarding the right to disclose confidential information. The changed text removes this warranty and replaces it with a statement of 'no guarantees'. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that Phoenix and Company agrees to hold confidential information in confidence and not disclose it without authorization. By removing the warranty, the changed text creates uncertainty about the responsibilities regarding confidential information, leading to a conflict with the original agreement to protect and keep it confidential as dictated by the contradicted section.

perturbation.original_text: Phoenix and Company agree to maintain as Confidential Information, the existence of these discussions regarding a possible business relationship, until an agreement is completed.
perturbation.changed_text: Phoenix and Company are free to disclose the existence of these discussions.
perturbation.explanation: Changing this clause creates a contradiction. Initially, the existence of business discussions was considered confidential. This revision now allows for disclosure, contradicting the spirit of the mutual non-disclosure agreement. It specifically contradicts the overall intent established in the 'Recitals' section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: WHEREAS, Phoenix and Company mutually desire to engage in discussions concerning a possible business relationship for the development and/or licensing of software products and, in furtherance of those discussions may find it necessary and advantageous to disclose to each other, certain confidential information regarding software products and strategic plans; and WHEREAS, Company and Phoenix consider such documents, records and information pertaining to products confidential and do not want them disclosed to third parties;
perturbation.justification: The original text maintained confidentiality of business discussions until an agreement was completed. The changed text explicitly allows disclosure of these discussions. This directly contradicts the contradicted text (Recitals) where both parties agree to keep confidential information pertaining to products and strategic plans secret from third parties. The change directly opposes the initially agreed-upon confidentiality, fitting the definition of 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' because it alters essential language, creating a contradiction with constraints stated in other sections.

perturbation.original_text: 3. Each party shall exercise such care in the protection of the confidential information of the other as they exercise in the protection of confidential information of their own.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Each party shall exercise reasonable care in the protection of the confidential information of the other.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'as they exercise in the protection of confidential information of their own.' from the paragraph and just leaving 'reasonable care', means a change in the 'standard of care'. The original text required parties to treat the others information as carefully as their own, implying a high bar. Changing to 'reasonable care' introduce ambiguity. It contradicts implicit expectation created by the term 'mutual' in the agreement's title, implying a lack of equal protection. In the original text, it could be argued that because it had a high bar of care, the disclosure of confidential information could be argued that it falls for an intent. However, with the removal of the original context, and replacing it with 'reasonable care' the argument can be different.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Phoenix and Company agree that they shall hold in confidence and shall not disclose any Confidential Information (as defined in Paragraph 2 below) without the prior written authorization from a corporate officer of the party to whom the information belongs nor use such Confidential Information for any purpose other than that contemplated by this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text imposed a higher standard of care by requiring each party to protect the other's confidential information as they protect their own. The changed text lowers this standard to 'reasonable care,' introducing ambiguity. This contradicts the contradicted text, which states that both parties 'shall hold in confidence and shall not disclose' confidential information, suggesting a higher, more stringent obligation than 'reasonable care' implies. The change therefore weakens the obligation related to confidential information, creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 4. Rights and obligations of this Agreement shall be binding upon the heirs, assigns and successors of Phoenix and Company.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. Rights and obligations of this Agreement shall be binding upon Phoenix and Company only.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'heirs, assigns and successors of', the contract's obligations are strictly limited to the original parties. This creates potential contradictions if either company is acquired or undergoes significant structural changes. It makes it unclear how the NDA would be enforced in such scenarios, contradicting the long-term intent typically associated with confidentiality agreements, and the intent in the 'recitals' of wanting information and documents not disclosed to third parties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: WHEREAS, Phoenix and Company mutually desire to engage in discussions concerning a possible business relationship for the development and/or licensing of software products and, in furtherance of those discussions may find it necessary and advantageous to disclose to each other, certain confidential information regarding software products and strategic plans; and WHEREAS, Company and Phoenix consider such documents, records and information pertaining to products confidential and do not want them disclosed to third parties;
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 4, by limiting the agreement's binding to only 'Phoenix and Company,' directly contradicts the 'WHEREAS' clause which outlines the intention to protect confidential information pertaining to products from being disclosed to third parties. The original text included 'heirs, assigns and successors,' providing a broader scope of responsibility. Removing this allows for the possibility that if Phoenix or Company undergo structural changes (acquisition, merger), the agreement's obligations may not extend to the new entity, which breaks the NDA's intent laid out in the contradicted text where disclosure of information to third parties should not occur. This meets the Omissions - In Text Contradiction definition.
